query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.92 (commit: 70eb2d9f).

## Issue to Resolve

[Jetpack Social] Fix share limits being shown for Atomic sites
Share limits shouldn't be shown for Atomic sites in the context of Jetpack Social. There are 3 place to double-check using an Atomic site:

1 - Share limits dashboard card;
2 - Edit post - publish bottom sheet;
3 - Edit post - settings;

See p9F6qB-dLk-p2#comment-56603

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

@RenanLukas Is there a timeline for when this might be fixed that we can share with the user? The ticket is still open and it would be nice if we could give them an update.
@bobmatyas we're going to have a look this week.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/usecase/social/GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’package org.wordpress.android.usecase.social
 2â†’
 3â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 4â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 5â†’import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore.FetchedJetpackSocialResult
 6â†’import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.doesNotContain
 7â†’import javax.inject.Inject
 8â†’
 9â†’class GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase @Inject constructor(
10â†’    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
11â†’) {
12â†’    suspend fun execute(siteModel: SiteModel): ShareLimit {
13â†’        val isShareLimitEnabled =
14â†’            !siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom
15â†’                    && (siteModel.planActiveFeatures?.split(",")?.doesNotContain(FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000) != false)
16â†’        val result = siteStore.fetchJetpackSocial(siteModel)
17â†’        return if (isShareLimitEnabled && result is FetchedJetpackSocialResult.Success) {
18â†’            with(result.jetpackSocial) {
19â†’                ShareLimit.Enabled(
20â†’                    shareLimit = shareLimit,
21â†’                    publicizedCount = publicizedCount,
22â†’                    sharedPostsCount = sharedPostsCount,
23â†’                    sharesRemaining = sharesRemaining,
24â†’                )
25â†’            }
26â†’        } else {
27â†’            ShareLimit.Disabled
28â†’        }
29â†’    }
30â†’}
31â†’
32â†’sealed interface ShareLimit {
33â†’    data class Enabled(
34â†’        val shareLimit: Int,
35â†’        val publicizedCount: Int,
36â†’        val sharedPostsCount: Int,
37â†’        val sharesRemaining: Int,
38â†’    ) : ShareLimit
39â†’
40â†’    object Disabled : ShareLimit
41â†’}
42â†’
43â†’private const val FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000 = "social-shares-1000"
44â†’

content.thinking:
**Clarifying atomic site detection**

I'm verifying whether atomic sites count as hosted at WPCom by inspecting code usage of the hosting flag, since SiteModel lacks clear info and atomic may be tracked externally. This affects share limit disabling logic.

**Clarifying atomic site flag usage**

I confirmed that "atomic" corresponds to sites marked as automated transfer, so disabling share limits requires checking siteModel.isAutomatedTransfer since isHostedAtWPCom alone is false for Atomic sites. Next, I'll verify how isAutomatedTransfer is used elsewhere to integrate this extra condition.

 16â†’    private val siteStore: SiteStore = mock()
 17â†’    private val classToTest = GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase(
 18â†’        siteStore = siteStore,
 19â†’    )
 20â†’
 21â†’    @Test
 22â†’    fun `Should return Disabled if site is not self hosted`() = test {
 23â†’        val siteModel = SiteModel().apply {
 24â†’            siteId = 1L

content.thinking:
**Adjusting usecase logic without test changes**

I'm planning to refine the usecase logic to incorporate the new atomic condition without modifying existing tests, since only adding new test files is allowed. I'll confirm the dependencies like the hosted-host check but keep tests untouched.

{"file_path":"/project/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/usecase/social/GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase.kt","old_string":"        val isShareLimitEnabled =
            !siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom
                    && (siteModel.planActiveFeatures?.split(",")?.doesNotContain(FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000) != false)
","new_string":"        val isShareLimitEnabled =
            !siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom
                    && !siteModel.isWPComAtomic
                    && !siteModel.isPrivateWPComAtomic
                    && (siteModel.planActiveFeatures?.split(",")?.doesNotContain(FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000) != false)
"}
